original: St Felix IV begins his reign as Catholic Pope
King Aethelstan is the first southern English king to gain control of much of the north of Britain when various local kings accept his overlordship at Eamont, Cumbria
Crusaders capture Syria's harbor city of Tripoli
Minamoto Yoritomo becomes Seii Taishōgun, the first ruling shogun in the history of Japan and de facto ruler
The Nuremburg Chronicle, an illustrated history of the world, is first published in Latin in Nuremburg, compiled by Hartmann Schedel and published by Anton Koberger
French troops under Maarten van Rossem occupy Flanders
Kett's uprising occupies Norwich, England
"Trinity Church" (now Saint Basil's Cathedral) is consecrated in Moscow, Russia, built to commemorate defeat of the Mongols at battle of Kazan
Diego de Lando, Bishop of Yucatan, burns on a large pyre Maya religious objects and most of the last surviving books of bark outside the Franciscan convent of Maní
Ostrog Bible, the first printed Bible in a Slavic language, is published
New Amsterdam's governor buys Gull Island from Indians for cargo, renames it Oyster Island, later known as Ellis Island

Second Battle of the James River: Dutch fleet led by admirals Cornelis Evertsen the Youngest and Jacob Binckes wins a tactical victory against an improvised English squadron led by Thomas Gardiner and captures four English ships
Antonio Pignatelli elected as Pope Innocentius XII
Stanislaw Leszcynski becomes king of part of Poland
Ottoman army officer Al-Ḥusayn ibn ʿAlī proclaims himself Bey of Tunis, founding the Husainid Dynasty (dynasty rules till 1957)
Lorenzo Corsini chosen as Pope Clement XII
Citizens of Carlisle, Pennsylvania, pass a symbolic declaration of independence
Cossack leader Emilian Pugachevs army occupies Kazan
CaptainJames Cookdeparts Plymouth, England helming HMS Resolution, beginning his 3rd and final trip to the Pacific
First manned flight in Netherlands: Frenchman Jean-Pierre Blanchard lifts off in his gas balloon from Noordeinde Palace in The Hague
French Revolution: The Civil Constitution of the Clergy is adopted, putting the Catholic Church in France under the control of the state
Second Battle of Algeciras: British fleet beats French and Spanish (six days after losing the 1st Battle of Algeciras)
US forces led by General Hull invade Canada (War of 1812)
First flower show is held in Dannybrook, County Cork, Ireland
Karl Drais von Sauerbronn demonstrates bicycle course
The Dutch Second Chamber accepts the establishment of Provincial States
William Goodale patents paper bag manufacturing machines in Massachusetts
Federal troops occupy Helena, Arkansas
US Congress authorizes Medal of Honor

British forces invade the Waikato in New Zealand, home of the Māori King Movement, beginning a new phase of the New Zealand Wars between the colonial government and allied Māori tribes on one side and Māori on the other
Ontario Agricultural College founded
Yellow fever epidemic in New Orleans begins, resulting in an estimated 4,500 deaths in the city
Agricultural and industrial tariffs are introduced in Germany
First ocean pier in the US is completed in Washington, D.C.
Jean-Baptiste Marchand hoists French flag in Fashoda, Sudan
114°F (46°C) is recorded in Basin, Wyoming (state record)
In Germany a group of 104 aristocrats present a deceleration against dueling, though the tradition will go on
Striking Canadian salmon fishermen on the Pacific coast, resentful of the non-union Japanese who continue to fish, maroon and imprison 47
The British and Japanese renew their alliance (of January 1902) for 10 years and agree to provide mutual support if attacked by other power
16th Amendment was passed by congress (power to tax incomes)
First foreign feature film is exhibited in the US: "Queen Elizabeth" in New York City
150,000 Ulstermen gather and resolve to resist Irish Home Rule by force of arms as the British Liberals promise the Irish nationalists Home Rule, making civil war appear imminent
Joseph Dunn (14) is attacked by a shark, suffering non-fatal wounds in New Jersey, the final episode in a spate of shark attacks along the Jersey coast where 4 people died over 12 days

The third and fourth of four fatal Jersey Shore shark attacks occur at Matawan Creek when epileptic Lester Stilwell (11) is dragged into the creek, and Watson Fisher (24), believing Stilwell had a seizure, is bitten while retrieving the body and subsequently bleeds to death
The Bisbee Deportation occurs as vigilantes kidnap and deport nearly 1,300 striking miners and others from Bisbee, Arizona
Japanese battleship explodes in Bay of Tokayama, 500 killed
Lithuania and USSR sign peace treaty, Lithuania becomes independent republic
Cleveland Indians (9) and New York Yankees (7) combine for an American League record of 16 doubles
Guomindangleger draws against warlord Wu Peifu
45,715 fans in the 35,000-seat Sportsman's Park in St. Louis help cause many ground-rule doubles, 11 in the first game and 21 in the second game for a total of 32
Hedley Verity establishes a first-class cricket record by taking all 10 wickets for only 10 runs against Nottinghamshire on a pitch affected by a storm
Congress passes first minimum wage law (33 cents per hour)
Screen Actors Guild incorporates in Hollywood, with Ralph Morgan as their first president
US Disciplinary Barracks on Alcatraz Island abandoned
Willy de Supervise swims world record in 400 m (5:16.0)
Belgium recognizes Soviet Union
Tupolev ANT-25 non-stop flight from Moscow to San Jacinto, California
Frederick McKinley Jones receives a patent for an air conditioning unit for trucks, helping to change long-haul carriage of food and blood
Battle of Kolombangara (Second Battle of Gulf of Kula)
Eighth day of Battle of Kursk, USSR: Operation Kutuzov (Soviet counter-offensive begins)
National Committee Freies Deutschland forms
Soviet offensive at Orel
World War II: Battle of Prokhorovka - Russia defeats German forces in one of the largest ever tank battles
Theresienstadt Family camp disbands, with 4,000 people gased
Chicago Cubs beat Boston, 6-1 to halt Braves outfielder Tommy Holmes' modern-day record NL hitting streak at 37 games
Vance Dinges hits only Phillie pinch hit inside-the-park home run
First jets to fly across Atlantic (six RAF de Havilland Vampires)
Baseball owners agree to erect warning paths before each fence
Dutch KLM Constellation crashes near Bombay, killing 45
LA Rams sign Norm Van Brocklin
Hague Council of Annulment convicts German war criminals W Lages, FH Van de Funten and F Fischer to death
Mob tries to keep black family from moving into all-white Cicero, Illinois
KTVB TV channel 7 in Boise, ID (NBC) begins broadcasting
ANC President Albert Luthuli banned by South African Minister of Justice from attending public gatherings and confined to the magisterial district of Lower Tugela, Natal
Major League Baseball Players Association founded
Christian Democratic Party forms in Argentina
Dwight D. Eisenhowerbecomes the first President to fly by helicopter
US Surgeon General Leroy Burney links smoking to lung cancer
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Bikini Island
NBC uses cameras to show catchers signals during Yankee-Red Sox game
Orlyonok, the main Young Pioneer camp of the Russian SFSR, is founded
USSR's Sputnik 5 launches with two dogs
XEWT TV Channel 12 in Tijuana-San Diego, CA (IND) begins broadcasting
First two-manned craft in space (USSR)
Rolling Stones' first performance at the Marquee Club in London
10.51 inches (26.70 cm) of rainfall in Sandusky, Ohio (state record, until broken in 1995)
Start of 3-day race riot in Chicago; looting brings out National Guardsmen
US Treasury announces it will buy mutilated silver coins at silver bullion price at Philadelphia and Denver mints
Greek regime deprives 480 Greeks of their citizenship
Race riot in Newark, New Jersey, results in 26 deaths, 1,500 injuries, and over 1,000 arrests
Couve de Murville forms government in France
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
During the "marching season," serious rioting occurs in Derry, Belfast, and Dungiven, forcing many families in Belfast to leave their homes
Tanzania signs contract with China for building Tanzam-railway between Tanzania and Zambia
Juan Corona, indicted for 25 murders

Twelve years after the banning of the ANC and Pan Africanist Congress, a new political movement, the Black People Convention is formed after a three day long conference in Pietermaritzburg, South Africa
A fire destroys the entire sixth floor of the National Personnel Records Center of the United States
John Ehrlichman convicted of violating Daniel Ellsberg's rights
England cricket wicketkeeper Bob Taylor catches seven in an innings for Derbyshire vs. Yorkshire at Queen's Park, Chesterfield
Sao Tomé e Príncipe gains independence from Portugal (National Day)
First "Family Feud" game show debuts on ABC hosted by Richard Dawson
John Edrich scores his 100th century for Surrey against Derbyshire at The Oval
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Deep Purple drummer Ian Paice joins Whitesnake
Kiribati (formerly Gilbert Islands) declares independence from the United Kingdom
Mafia head of the Bonanno family, Carmine Galante dies with a cigar in his mouth, assassinated by the Mafia commission after he declared himself “boss of bosses” of the New York underworld
Britain announces it is returning 593 Argentine POWs
FEMA promises survivors of a nuclear war will receive their mail
Chad government troops reconquer Abéché
A car bomb set off by the military wing of the ANC, explodes in Durban South Africa killing 5 and injuring 27 people
STS 51-F launch is scrubbed at T-3 seconds due to main engine shutdown
Steven McDonald, New York City Police Department patrolman, is shot and paralyzed by Shavod Jones
Fifty white South Africans meet ANCers in Dakar
First time in 20 years a delegation from USSR lands in Israel
Phillies' Kent Tekulve pitches his 900th game in relief
Margo Adams alleges Red Sox Wade Bogg's had an affair with her
USSR launches Phobos II for Martian orbit
Chicago White Sox Melido Perez no-hits Yankees 8-0 in a rain-shortened six-inning game at Yankee Stadium (seventh no-hitter of 1990)
In Soweto, South Africa, Shanty town women strip to the waist and confront bulldozers sent by authorities to demolish their homes
7.8 earthquake hits Hokkaido Japan, killing 160
Talk radio hostDon Imusbegins broadcasting to Boston on WEEI (590 AM)
Nomination hearings for Stephen Breyer for Supreme Court Justice begin
Start of the first "Super 8s" tournament in Kuala Lumpur
Cubs play in their 5,000th consecutive game without being no-hit
Pittsburgh Pirates duo Francisco Cordova and Ricardo Rincon no-hit the Houston Astros 3-0 in 10 innings at Three Rivers Stadium
Hezbollah initiates Operation True Promise
Canadian harness racer John Campbell wins his 10,000th race as a driver by guiding 'Share the Delight' to victory in the 6th race at Meadowlands Racetrack
200 people are killed by the Syrian army in Tremseh
90-155 people are killed after an oil tanker crashes and explodes in Okogbe, Rivers State, Nigeria
Eight people are killed after a commuter train derails in Paris

World premiere of Richard Blackford and Bernie Krause's "The Great Animal Orchestra Symphony," combining natural recordings of animals, including gibbons, whales, birds, and frogs, with a traditional classical orchestra, by the BBC National Orchestra of Wales led by Martyn Brabbins at the Cheltenham Festival in England
World's largest iceberg (later christened A68) breaks away from Larsen C Ice Shelf in Antarctica, about 6,000 sq km in length
Chemical plant explosion in Jiangan County, Sichuan Province, China, kills at least 19
Republic of Ireland will be the first country to sell its investments in fossil fuel companies after passing legislation in parliament
World's longest fingernails are cut off; Indian man Shridhar Chillal's left-hand nails measured a combined total of 909.6 cm (358.1 inches) after growing for 66 years
At least 92 people die in a fire in a COVID-19 hospital ward in Iraqi city of Nasiriya
Netflix's South Korean drama "Squid Game" is the first non-English series to be nominated for an Emmy for Outstanding Drama
NATO summit ends in Vilnius, Lithuania, with Turkey agreeing to Sweden's membership and Ukraine membership “when allies agree and conditions are met”
New Caledonia is declared a state by France in a historic autonomy deal but remains within the Republic
Third Crusade: City of Acre surrenders to English KingRichard Ithe Lionheart and the Crusaders after a siege of nearly two years
KingAlfonso Vof Aragon becomes King of Naples
Battle of Albancay:Diego de Almagrodefeated by army led by Alonso de Alvarado on behalf ofFrancisco Pizarro
English invasion fleet underGeorge Villierslands on the Île de Ré, forced to withdraw after 3 months of combat and losing 4,000 of 7,000 troops
Britain's KingCharles IIratifies Habeas Corpus Act allowing prisoners' right to a court review of the justification for their imprisonment
Battle of Aughrim (Aghrim) Ireland,William IIIbeatsJames II
HMS Endeavourcaptained byJames Cook, withJoseph Banksaboard, returns from its first voyage to the Pacific after two years
Former United States Secretary of the TreasuryAlexander Hamiltondies after being shot in a pistol duel the previous day by Vice PresidentAaron Burr, near Weehaken, New Jersey
Mormon leaderJoseph Smithsays God allows polygamy
"Wild Bill" Hickok legend is born as he reputedly shoots and kills David McCanles in Rock Creek, Nebraska Territory; Hickok later acquitted of murder as court finds he acted in self defense.
MLB Boston Americans pitcherCy Youngwins his 300th baseball game
Arthur BalfoursucceedsLord Salisbury, who retired as British Prime Minister on 11 July
The Dreyfus Affair:Alfred Dreyfusis found innocent of treason in France
Paavo Nurmiwalks world record 4 x 1500 m (16:26.2)
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruth, halfway to his MLB record of 60 home runs, hits #30 off Joe Shaute in the 9th inning in New York's 7-0 win over the Cleveland Indians at Dunn Field in Cleveland
PopePius XIIreceives German ambassador baron von Weizsacker
US government recognizes authority of French GeneralCharles de Gaulle
Benjamin Britten's "Rape of Lucretia" premieres at Glyndebourne Opera Festival
NY Yankees pitcherAllie Reynoldsthrows no-hitter in Cleveland against the Indians, his former team, in a 1-0 win
PresidentEisenhowerput forward a plan for an interstate highway system
Thor Heyerdahlcrosses the Atlantic ocean on the raft Ra II, arrives in Barbados from Morocco after 57 days
Democrats nominateGeorge McGovernfor US president in Miami, Florida
Ian Duryand the Kilburns disband
Geraldine Ferrarobecomes the first US female major-party vice-presidential candidate afterWalter Mondaleselects her as his Democratic Party running mate
Doctors discover a cancerous growth in PresidentRonald Reagan's colon
NY Yankee pitching greatRon Guidryretires (170-91 .651, 3.29 ERA)
Boris Yeltsinquits the Soviet Communist Party
Axl Roseis arrested on riot charges in St. Louis stemming from a concert on July 2
Andrew Lloyd Webber's musical "Sunset Boulevard," based onBilly Wilder's 1950 film, starring Patti Lupone and Daniel Benzali, opens at the Adelphi Theatre, London
Enrique Iglesiasreleases his first album, "Enrique Iglesias"
Kirby Puckett, MLB centerfielder, retires after 12 seasons from Minnesota Twins, due to loss of vision in one eye
Michael Jordansigns a NBA contract with the Chicago Bulls, for 1 year for $30.1 million
Prison drama "Oz" created by Tom Fontana, starring J.K. Simmons andChristopher Meloni, debuts on HBO as the channel's first original drama series
FIFA World Cup Final, Stade de France, Saint-Denis:Zinedine Zidanescores twice as France wins first World Cup beating Brazil, 3-0
South African PresidentNelson MandelaaccompaniesQueen Elizabeth IIon a coach drive through the streets of London
Hindi film "Devdas" premieres directed by Sanjay Leela Bhansali and starringShah Rukh Khan,Aishwarya Raiand Madhuri Dixit (Best Film Filmfare Awards)
Malala Yousafzaiaddresses the United Nations and calls for worldwide access to education
Bernie Sandersendorses fellow Democratic Presidential candidateHillary Clintonin a speech in New Hampshire
"Mission: Impossible - Fallout", directed by Christopher McQuarrie and starringTom Cruise,Henry Cavill, Ving Rhames,Simon PeggandRebecca Ferguson, premieres in Paris
Sandra Ohis the first woman of Asian descent to be nominated for an Emmy Award in a leading role for "Killing Eve"
US PresidentDonald Trumparrives in the UK for a 4-day visit amid protests
Twitter suesElon Muskafter he pulls out of a deal to buy the social media site, accusing him of "trashing it" and "a model of bad faith"
Gelderland accepts Gregorian calendar; yesterday is June 30, 1700
"Disco Demolition Night" at Comiskey Park: fans go wild destroying disco records and cause the White Sox to forfeit second game of a doubleheader to the Detroit Tigers
Ashikaga Yoshinori, 6th Japanese shōgun of the Ashikaga shogunate (1428-41), born in Japan (d. 1441)
